rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WON FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Arlene Jeungs Notice Of Demurrer To Donna Ings First Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: Demurrer to Count Seven (Constructive Fraud) and Count Eight (Fraud) of the First Amended Petition is sustained with leave to amend. Respondent is not correct that the elements of fraud cannot be pleaded on information and belief and a petition is subject to demurrer merely on those grounds alone. A fraud claim may be pleaded on information and belief so long as the facts upon which the belief is founded are stated in the pleading. The Petition states facts upon which Petitioner's belief is based. However, the Petition does not show a prima facie foundation for the fraud claim; there is no causal link pleaded between the alleged misrepresentation and the alleged damage to the Trust. The pleaded facts in support of the fraud claim also lack the specificity as to time and content exampled in Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Goods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, and Tenet Healthsystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of California (2016) 245 Cal. App. 4th 821. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, February 20, 2019.
